meta.title: human-humanoid-tools (hhtools)
meta.description: Retarget parkour, dance, and interaction clips onto any humanoid in ~30 seconds

hero.title: human-humanoid-tools
hero.subtitle: Retarget parkour, dance, and interaction clips onto any humanoid in ~30 seconds
hero.requirements: Linux · Python 3.12+ · Preview on CPU · Retarget needs NVIDIA GPU (CUDA 12)

fastRetarget.title: Fast Retarget
fastRetarget.desc: Drag a human clip, pick a robot, export CSV/ZIP in about 30 seconds. Dual backends: Newton IK for pure skeleton mimic (dance, walk) and MPC-SQP Interaction-Mesh for object/terrain interaction.
bullets: Auto backend recommendation by motion type

anyMotion.title: Any Motion
anyMotion.desc: BVH / GLB / SMPL family and NPZ — plus interaction clips. Adapters for AMASS, GVHMR, LAFAN, OMOMO, PHUMA, intermimic, meshmimic, and more.
bullets: Unified NPZ schema for pipeline interchange

anyUrdf.title: Any URDF
anyUrdf.desc: Upload any robot in the Web UI: drag in the URDF, drag in meshes. The system auto-scaffolds robot.yaml — no manual IK tuning required.
bullets: One-time calibration aligns robot to reference skeleton
Presets for Unitree G1 and more

batchRetarget.title: Batch Retarget
batchRetarget.desc: Queue multiple clips from the motion library or uploads, run retarget in one pass, and download a ZIP of robot trajectories.
bullets: Multi-select from library or folder import

r2r.title: Robot → Robot (R2R)
r2r.desc: Retarget existing robot CSV/PKL exports onto a new URDF. Single-clip and batch ZIP workflows with dual-view comparison.

datasetViz.title: Dataset Analysis & Visualize
datasetViz.desc: Scan, tag, embed, cluster, and subset human or robot motion libraries in the Web UI. Explore quality/dynamics tags and scatter plots; export recommended subsets.

video.placeholderHint: Place MP4 at the path shown in docs/assets/videos/README.md

workflow.title: Recommended Workflow
workflow.subtitle: Seven steps from clip import to export

steps.title: Import Motion
steps.desc: Load BVH, SMPL, OMOMO PKL, meshmimic terrain, or pick from the built-in library.

steps.title: Motion Library
steps.desc: Browse assets/motions by folder; search and one-click load demo clips.

steps.title: Load Robot
steps.desc: Drag URDF + meshes or select a registered robot preset.

steps.title: Calibrate
steps.desc: Align the gray robot to the blue reference skeleton; save once per robot.

steps.title: Retarget
steps.desc: Choose Newton IK or Interaction-Mesh; run retarget in ~30 seconds.

steps.title: 3D Visualization
steps.desc: Toggle skeleton, body, objects/terrain, scaled scene, and robot layers.

steps.title: Export
steps.desc: Download CSV or ZIP with robot and interaction object trajectories.

method.title: Method
method.subtitle: Two retarget backends under one pipeline

newton.title: Newton IK (mimic)
newton.desc: GPU-accelerated inverse kinematics for pure skeleton motions — dance, locomotion, and general mimic clips without interaction geometry.

mesh.title: Interaction-Mesh (intermimic / meshmimic)
mesh.desc: Laplacian deformation on an interaction mesh, solved with MPC/SQP. Preserves spatial relationships between body and objects/terrain while respecting non-penetration.

pipeline.title: Unified Pipeline
pipeline.desc: All inputs convert to a canonical Motion representation, pass through calibration, then export robot CSV/PKL with optional object and terrain assets.

datasets.title: Supported Datasets
datasets.subtitle: Demo paths only — download full datasets from upstream

citation.title: Citation & License
citation.licenseText: Code: Apache-2.0. SMPL weights not included — register at MPI. When publishing, also cite upstream datasets and solvers (see NOTICE).
